name: Chocolate Pistachio Orange-Loaf
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. In a large bowl, combine sugar and butter. Beat at medium speed until creamy (1-2 minutes).
3. Add eggs; continue beating until well mixed (1-2 minutes).
4. Add light sour cream, grated orange peel, and orange extract. Continue beating, scraping the bowl often, until creamy (about 1 minute).
5. Gradually add flour, baking powder, salt, and baking soda. Beat until just mixed (about 1 minute). Do not overmix.
6. By hand, stir chocolate chips and chopped pistachios into the batter.
7. Spoon batter into a greased 9-inch x 5-inch x 3-inch loaf pan.
8. Bake for 60-70 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean. If browning too quickly, cover the loaf with aluminum foil.
9. Cool for 10 minutes; then invert onto a cooling rack and cool completely.
10. Stir glaze ingredients together until smooth; drizzle over bread.
Note: For a more tender crust, do not glaze immediately. Wrap the loaf in plastic wrap and refrigerate overnight; then prepare the glaze and drizzle over the bread.
